seo.title: Falls Management for Assisted Living | Able Care
seo.description: Reduce the fall burden on staff. Protect length of stay. Defend against liability. Objective screening in under five minutes per resident.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Nurse handing an assessment kit to a care team member
blocks.headline: Falls management for assisted living.
blocks.subtitle: Reduce the fall burden on staff. Protect length of stay. Defend against liability. Objective screening in under five minutes.

blocks.heading: The fall burden in assisted living
blocks.body:
Assisted living staff are stretched. Acuity is rising. Falls generate incident reports, family complaints, regulatory scrutiny and, increasingly, litigation.

Paper-based screening tools add documentation time without improving outcomes. They are subjective, inconsistent across staff and difficult to defend in a liability context.

Able Assess replaces paper tools with four objective, validated metrics captured in under five minutes. CNAs and med techs can run the workflow. The data is standardized, timestamped and defensible.

blocks.heading: How it works in assisted living

steps.title: Screen at intake
steps.description: Capture a baseline within 48 hours of move-in. Four validated metrics in a single five-minute session.

steps.title: Stratify risk
steps.description: Residents are banded by fall risk using normative data. High-risk residents are flagged for the DON and care team immediately.

steps.title: Monitor quarterly
steps.description: Repeat screenings track functional change over time. Declining scores trigger care plan updates before a fall occurs.

steps.title: Report and defend
steps.description: Standardized, timestamped records create a defensible audit trail for regulatory reviews and liability enquiries.

blocks.heading: Four metrics that protect your community

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whyItMatters: A validated predictor of hospitalization. Declining grip strength in AL residents signals functional change that requires intervention.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whyItMatters: Transfer-related falls are among the most common in assisted living. This metric identifies residents at highest risk.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: Walking speed over a short distance.
metrics.whyItMatters: Declining gait speed is a reliable early warning. It gives care teams time to intervene before a fall triggers a move or a claim.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whyItMatters: Gives the DON objective, population-level data on fall risk across the entire community. Defensible and repeatable.

blocks.heading: Delegate screening, not responsibility
blocks.body: Able Assess enables CNAs and med techs to run standardized screenings. The DON retains oversight through a dashboard that surfaces declining residents and population-level trends without adding to their workload.
blocks.imageAlt: Clinician and patient using Able Assess on a tablet during a screening session

blocks.heading: Model the impact of fewer falls
blocks.promoBody: Falls in assisted living drive hospitalization, liability claims and early move-outs. See what a 25% reduction could mean for your community.

testimonials.quote: The data is timestamped and objective. When a family or attorney asks what we did to prevent a fall, we can show them exactly what we documented and when.
The time from incident to intervention dropped significantly. We are catching decline earlier and acting on it faster.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to assisted living

items.question: Can CNAs and med techs run the screening?
items.answer: Yes. Able Assess was designed for frontline staff. CNAs, med techs and care aides can all run the five-minute workflow after a short onboarding session. No clinical certifications are required.

items.question: Our residents have higher acuity than IL. Does the screening still work?
items.answer: Yes. The four metrics are validated across a wide range of functional ability, including residents with mobility aids and cognitive impairment. The protocol adapts to the resident's capacity.

items.question: How does it help with liability?
items.answer: A standardized, timestamped screening record demonstrates proactive falls risk management. This is significantly more defensible than subjective clinical notes or paper-based tools.

items.question: Does it integrate with our EHR?
items.answer: Able Assess supports data export and integration with major EHR platforms. Assessment results can be pushed into the resident record to support care planning and compliance documentation.

items.question: Can it be used in memory care settings?
items.answer: Yes. The assessment protocol has been used successfully with residents who have mild to moderate cognitive impairment. Staff guide the resident through each metric using simple, consistent prompts.

items.question: How does pricing work for points-based or bundled billing models?
items.answer: Pricing is based on community size and assessment volume, not billing model. The ROI calculator can model payback against your specific revenue and cost structure. Book a demo to discuss your scenario.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in an assisted living workflow.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has deployed it in assisted living communities.
